title: The Material Cost Law
subtitle: Half price, paid in components and calendar

content.text: Procurement desk. List price is for people who only bring money.

content.label: THE LAW
content.text: Making, repairing, or installing it yourself costs 50% of list price, paid in components — and requires the capability, the stated time, and the components. All three. Every time.

The capability. A feature, perk, or facility that says you can: Crafting features (The Twelve Skills), a Tinkerer's bench (Tinkerer), a Workshop (The Base). No capability, no discount — that's what the trades are selling.

The stated time. The calendar is the real tax. Crafting periods compete with training, treatment, and profession work — the 50% discount is purchased with downtime that could have become anything else.
The components. Half of list, in parts. Where the parts come from is the next section, and it is most of the game.

Domes sell components at price; margins make sourcing them fiction. Inside the wall, the 50% law is a shopping trip. Outside it, the components rate is the start of a story — legwork Checks, Streetwise, the gray market, a Fixer who knows a guy. The margin crafter's real cost is the finding.

Salvage substitutes. Salvaged and harvested components substitute at GM-assessed value — loot is liquidity. Carve yields are components (hides to the armor table, chitin to the bench, glands to the pharmacy — see Carving). Assess the value, apply it against the components bill, and the corpse becomes the discount.

Licensed designs self-crafted at 50% assume you hold the license. Without it, you are producing bootleg-grade work, drawbacks included — the discount doesn't launder the provenance. (License prices are named but never costed — flagged on Patterns.)

content.label: THE MARGIN'S FULL STACK
content.text: Bootleg recipe + your own press = a quarter of corp list — and it jams on a natural 1. That is the whole bargain, printed where both parties can read it: the corp's price buys the corp's reliability, and the margin's price buys the margin's.
Armor repair and reforge — the break economy prices by this law (see Armor; the trade — see Tinkerer, whose field patches run material cost +50%).
The bench — mods, consumables, handloads, installs (Crafting — see The Twelve Skills; catalogs on Mods and Consumables & Ammo).
The pharmacy — a Street Doc's Pharmacopoeia crafts antidotes and stims at material cost (see Street Doc).
Bootleg patterns — pressing your own is a crafting question, not a casting one; the jam risk above is the casting side's inheritance (see Patterns).
Hide armors — carve grades enter the crafting table as components (see Carving).

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: The law requires "the stated time," but no catalog prints per-item crafting or repair times. And weapon and mod list prices are themselves unprinted (flagged on Weapons and Mods) — 50% of an unprinted number is still unprinted.
VENDOR NOTICE: Components purchased through authorized dome outlets include full provenance documentation. Components purchased elsewhere include a story. Auditors price both.
